entities.name: MILLET
entities.description: Grains recognized for their health and nutritional values, including sorghum, which can improve blood hemoglobin concentration.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A specific type of millet included in the investigation of hemoglobin concentration.

entities.name: HEMOGLOBIN CONCENTRATION
entities.description: The level of hemoglobin in the blood, which is positively affected by millet consumption.

entities.name: ANEMIA
entities.description: A major public health issue characterized by low hemoglobin levels.

entities.name: SYSTEMATIC REVIEW
entities.description: A research method used to investigate the effect of millet consumption on hemoglobin concentration.

entities.name: META-ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to synthesize data from multiple randomized control trials to determine the effect of millet on hemoglobin.

entities.name: RANDOMIZED CONTROL TRIALS
entities.description: The design of the published studies used as data for the meta-analysis.

entities.name: DIFFERENCE-IN-DIFFERENCES ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical analysis used to reveal the positive effects of millet consumption on hemoglobin concentration.

entities.name: IRON INTAKE
entities.description: The process of consuming iron, which is likely responsible for the increase in hemoglobin concentration.

entities.name: IRON CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of iron present in different types of millets.

entities.name: BIOAVAILABILITY
entities.description: The degree to which iron from millets is absorbed and utilized by the body.

entities.name: CHILDREN
entities.description: The demographic group that showed a change from mild anemia to normal status upon millet consumption.
